objective: Implement one owned stdlib-only fail-closed authority verifier that pins trusted issuer identities and classes and resolves complete PolicySnapshot and ApprovalReceipt objects before admission or resume can mutate the canonical backend.
executable_blocker: Independent Agent 1 and Agent 5 probes accepted self-issued JobSpec Permit and AttemptLease objects, an unresolved arbitrary PolicySnapshot hash and a fabricated nonempty resume approval string at the last audited canonical head.

required: one owned verifier boundary with explicit immutable trust configuration
missing verifier resolver trust record or resolved object fails closed before ledger or control backend mutation
JobSpec Permit AttemptLease PolicySnapshot and ApprovalReceipt issuer id and authority class are pinned
PolicySnapshot is fully resolved integrity checked active and exactly bound to Permit before claim
ApprovalReceipt is fully resolved integrity checked unexpired unrevoked action-bound and policy-bound before resume
the current valid synthetic offline reference semantics remain available through an explicit trusted fixture
forbidden: boolean callback prevalidated result default allow or caller self-asserted trust
unresolved policy or approval reference acceptance
cryptographic authenticity claim without a frozen key and signature contract
ledger contract schema domain registry runner or IPC transport change

acceptance_commands: exact write-set dependency private-locator network live-authority skip and weakening scan
rollback: Agent 0 discards the single worker commit and deletes the isolated branch/worktree. The canonical ledger, frozen contracts, policy sources, domain registries, runtime state and external systems remain unchanged.
